message:
chore(beads): close bd-73gu and absorb concurrent beads updates

Closes bd-73gu (metamorphic proptests for mutex_contract_transition)
with the 5-MR summary pointing at commits a41297b/790211a/0cf160b/e448ca0.

Also absorbs unrelated beads records produced concurrently by other
swarm agents into the shared JSONL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3ue8","title":"Conformance: execute math_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for math_ops.json cases in the harness, using numeric tolerance rather than exact string comparison for floating outputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:37:26.365499536Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:43:53.447382761Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:43:53.445426066Z","close_reason":"Added direct math_ops fixture executor coverage with numeric tolerance and verified focused harness test/check/clippy through rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","math","testing"]}
 {"id": "bd-3v3", "title": "Harness: asupersync deterministic orchestration for conformance + runtime_math scenarios", "description": "Integrate /dp/asupersync into glibc-rs-harness runner.

Scope:
- Deterministic scheduling, traceability ids, structured results.
- Orchestrate scenario runs that exercise runtime_math controllers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness runner uses asupersync primitives for reproducible runs and evidence logging.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CobaltForge", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:35:42.149982753Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:29.149784585Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:29.149765789Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "comments": [{"id": 1022, "issue_id": "bd-3v3", "author": "CobaltForge", "text": "Implemented asupersync-backed deterministic conformance orchestration in glibc-rs-harness.

- Added `glibc_rs_harness::asupersync_orchestrator` (feature `asupersync-tooling`) to run fixture verification deterministically (stable fixture-set + case ordering), assign stable `trace_id`s, and emit structured evidence via `asupersync_conformance::{SuiteResult, TestResult, Checkpoint, ConformanceTestLogger}`.
- Fixed harness runner bug where fixture cases with `mode=both` were executed with mode=both (unsupported) instead of the active runner mode.
- `harness verify` now sorts fixture JSON paths deterministically and uses the orchestrator when `asupersync-tooling` is enabled; when `--report` is provided it additionally writes `<report>.suite.json` via `asupersync_conformance::write_json_report`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets` all pass.
", "created_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:20Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-3vtx", "title": "bd-1rf subtask: hosts files lookup subset implementation + explicit scope boundaries", "description": "Background:
- hosts lookups are a practical dependency for many workloads and must be deterministic in files-backend scope.

Goal:
- Implement hosts files lookup subset with explicit scope boundaries and error semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Deterministic hosts parsing and lookup.
2) API mapping for supported resolver-facing calls in files scope.
3) Explicit non-goals for networked NSS backends.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Covered host lookup fixtures pass.
- Out-of-scope behavior is explicit and documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for IPv4/IPv6 and malformed hosts entries.
- Structured logs: trace_id, query, family, result_source, outcome, timing.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CloudyRobin", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:01:53.995225917Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-15T19:03:24.895427810Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-15T19:02:54.796760654Z", "close_reason": "Implemented and verified (hosts files subset)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "nss", "resolver", "testing"], "comments": [{"id": 1023, "issue_id": "bd-3vtx", "author": "CloudyRobin", "text": "Implementation + verification evidence for `bd-3vtx`:

Implemented scope (files backend only; explicit non-goal for DNS/networked NSS fallback):
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs`
  - added `getaddrinfo_with_hosts(...)`
  - retained numeric resolution path
  - added deterministic hosts-content lookup path and family filtering
  - explicit no-network fallback behavior (`EAI_NONAME` when unresolved in files subset)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`
  - wired `/etc/hosts` subset resolution in ABI `getaddrinfo` for non-numeric names
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs`
  - conformance executor supports optional `hosts_content` input for `getaddrinfo`
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/resolver.json`
  - added `getaddrinfo_hosts_file_subset` fixture

Commits carrying this work:
- `2a6d8aa` feat(resolv): add /etc/hosts file-backend lookup for getaddrinfo
- `ebb96f5` test(conformance): add hosts-file getaddrinfo resolver fixture

Verification (offloaded using `rch`, remote worker `vmi1152480`):
1. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core getaddrinfo_with_hosts -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (4 tests)
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_ipv4_lookup`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_respects_family_filter`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_case_insensitive_alias`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_no_network_fallback`
2. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance execute_getaddrinfo_case_uses_hosts_subset_when_provided -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (1 test)

Operational note: an initial `rch` run in the active shared workspace hit rsync churn due concurrent file activity by other agents and attempted local fallback; I stopped those runs and re-ran in an isolated temporary worktree to keep execution fully remote/offloaded.
", "created_at": "2026-02-15T19:02:16Z"}]}

- Bench delta and update regression thresholds if justified.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "GentleOwl", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:32:26.472452403Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T18:52:52.602069532Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T18:52:52.602051328Z", "close_reason": "32 tests passing: unit tests for safe/unsafe points, property tests (monotone risk/depth/adverse sweeps, no-panic extremes), kernel integration, deterministic regression golden, hot-path integer-only evidence. 708 total tests, clippy clean.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-45d", "depends_on_id": "bd-19h", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-45d", "depends_on_id": "bd-242", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-45d", "depends_on_id": "bd-d5l", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4f09","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:24:26.481202683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:45:39.246535435Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:45:39.244569423Z","close_reason":"Removed stale replacement-guard exception after io_internal_abi switched to stdio_abi tmpfile/fclose; guard and standalone parity pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-6omr","title":"Execute memcpy_strict fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade memcpy_strict conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:47:59.373475315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T00:53:20.886987523Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T00:53:20.884896522Z","close_reason":"Upgraded memcpy_strict conformance coverage to execute every fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added top-level fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-6yd", "title": "Crash bundle: deterministic evidence + backtrace capture on failure", "description": "Critique mapping: #1 (prove; do not hand-wave).

Deliverables:
- When any smoke/fixture fails, capture:
  - backtrace (RUST_BACKTRACE=1)
  - mode + env vars
  - evidence ledger snapshot (TSM repairs/denials)
  - allocator stats snapshot (arena/quarantine)
  - /proc/self/maps excerpt for pointer provenance debugging

Acceptance:
- Bundle generation is deterministic and bounded (no unbounded logs).
- Bundle is sufficient to reproduce the failure locally.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): crash_bundle_test (9 pass) + gate script. Artifact filenames, bounds, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist all validated.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:37:26.930667114Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z", "close_reason": "Crash bundle specification operational. 9 harness tests pass. check_crash_bundle.sh validates evidence snapshots, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["alien", "critique", "diagnostics"]}
-{"id":"bd-73gu","title":"Metamorphic tests for pthread mutex contract state machine","description":"Add metamorphic relation (MR) proptests to frankenlibc-core::pthread::mutex for mutex_contract_transition. Gaps in existing coverage: (1) TryLock.blocks invariant (must always be false), (2) Lock+Unlock roundtrip identity from Unlocked, (3) blocks=>errno=0 implication, (4) N-cycle Lock/Unlock preserves Unlocked state, (5) Destroy from Locked* returns EBUSY preserving state. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex.rs #[cfg(test)] module. One MR per commit, each validated against existing passing behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:03:36.092304396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:03:41.872996371Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-73gu","title":"Metamorphic tests for pthread mutex contract state machine","description":"Add metamorphic relation (MR) proptests to frankenlibc-core::pthread::mutex for mutex_contract_transition. Gaps in existing coverage: (1) TryLock.blocks invariant (must always be false), (2) Lock+Unlock roundtrip identity from Unlocked, (3) blocks=>errno=0 implication, (4) N-cycle Lock/Unlock preserves Unlocked state, (5) Destroy from Locked* returns EBUSY preserving state. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex.rs #[cfg(test)] module. One MR per commit, each validated against existing passing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:03:36.092304396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:24.934564282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:24.932597224Z","close_reason":"Added 5 independent metamorphic proptests on mutex_contract_transition: (1) prop_trylock_never_blocks (FS×I/C≈20), (2) prop_lock_unlock_roundtrip_is_identity, (3) prop_trylock_unlock_roundtrip_is_identity, (4) prop_blocks_implies_success (structural invariant over full kind×state×op cube), (5) prop_destroyed_absorbs_non_init_ops + prop_uninitialized_absorbs_non_init_ops (trap-state fences). Mutation-validated MR1 by flipping blocks=false→true in LockedBySelf+TryLock+RECURSIVE arm — caught on first run. All 22 pthread::mutex tests pass. Commits: a41297b, 790211a, 0cf160b, e448ca0.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-73r", "title": "EPIC: Runtime Hot-Path Perf Gates (strict <20ns, hardened <200ns)", "description": "Goal: enforce extreme-software-optimization discipline for runtime_math.

Why:
- runtime_math is on the membrane hot path. Any regression here leaks into every libc call we gate.

Constraints:
- Strict mode must stay <20ns/call overhead target.
- Hardened mode must stay <200ns/call overhead target.
- No behavior regressions without explicit isomorphism proof + fixture evidence.

Outputs:
- Microbench + profile baselines for RuntimeMathKernel::decide + observe_validation_result.
- Golden snapshot outputs to detect accidental semantic drift.
- A repeatable baseline/profile/verify loop that future work MUST follow.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:30:15.483973301Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T01:28:13.676103762Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T01:28:04.198742106Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-73r", "depends_on_id": "bd-1om", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-73r", "depends_on_id": "bd-242", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-73r", "depends_on_id": "bd-38w", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-73r", "depends_on_id": "bd-3dv", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-73r", "depends_on_id": "bd-d5l", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-73r", "depends_on_id": "bd-pt6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-73r", "depends_on_id": "bd-vj3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1055, "issue_id": "bd-73r", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "## Epic Notes: Extreme-Optimization Discipline For runtime_math

This epic encodes the non-negotiable loop: **baseline -> profile -> prove -> implement -> verify -> repeat**.

### What We Are Protecting
runtime_math runs at the ABI boundary of libc call paths. Any accidental overhead shows up everywhere.

Budgets (from AGENTS.md):
- strict overhead target: <20ns/call (fast exits)
- hardened overhead target: <200ns/call (bounded heavy path)

### What Counts As “Hot Path”
- `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` on the strict path
- per-call evidence recording (if enabled)
- pointer-validation stage ordering / cached biases

### Hard Rules
- No transcendental math, matrix solves, or heap allocation on strict fast path.
- Any stochastic element must be deterministic from seed (replayable).
- Any “expensive” kernel must be cadence-driven (epoch update) not per call.

### Measurement Artifacts Required
- Criterion microbench for `decide` / `observe` (ns/op + percentiles)
- Profile recipe (flamegraph/perf) pinned to a reproducible command
- Golden snapshot outputs + sha256 regression gate
- Isomorphism proof template for policy changes (behavior invariants)

### Definition Of Done
- We can quantify overhead deltas per kernel addition.
- We can detect regressions in CI before merging.
- We have an explicit narrative for every optimization: what changed, why safe, how measured.", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:52:00Z"}, {"id": 1056, "issue_id": "bd-73r", "author": "CobaltCompass", "text": "EPIC CLOSED by CobaltCompass. All 7 sub-beads complete: (1) bd-d5l microbench (BlueLake), (2) bd-vj3 per-kernel bench suite (BrightMoose, verified CobaltCompass), (3) bd-1om golden snapshots (CobaltForge), (4) bd-pt6 profiling recipe (BlueLake), (5) bd-3dv hard rule audit (CobaltCompass - new enforcement script + invariant test), (6) bd-242 regression thresholds (BlueLake), (7) bd-38w isomorphism proof template (BrightMoose). Infrastructure delivered: scripts/hard_rule_audit.sh, scripts/perf_gate.sh, scripts/snapshot_gate.sh, runtime_math_bench + runtime_math_kernels_bench + perf_baseline.json.", "created_at": "2026-02-11T01:28:13Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-747", "title": "Stub wave 1: getenv/setenv/unsetenv + environment invariants", "description": "Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement getenv/setenv/unsetenv semantics with thread-safety policy and strict/hardened parity notes.
- Add fixture tests for overwrite flags, invalid names, and concurrent reads.

Acceptance:
- Environment fixtures match glibc behavior for covered cases.
- Symbols removed from top-stub list and support matrix upgraded accordingly.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:09.830918180Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "env", "stubs"]}
 {"id":"bd-761b","title":"Conformance: direct execute spawn_exec_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct execute_fixture_case coverage for spawn_exec_ops fixtures so posix_spawn, execve, and system cases are checked against the shared executor API directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T13:14:22.788257943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T13:22:56.014823852Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T13:22:56.012335843Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for spawn_exec_ops fixtures; verified focused test/check/clippy via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","process","testing"]}
@@ -788,5 +789,5 @@
 {"id": "bd-zh1y.7.2", "title": "Implement minimal UDP DNS stub resolver (A/AAAA records)", "description": "Parse /etc/resolv.conf for nameserver IPs. Construct DNS query packets (RFC 1035) for A and AAAA record types. Send via UDP socket to nameserver, parse response. Handle: CNAME indirection (1 level), timeout/retry (2 attempts, 5s timeout), search domain suffix from resolv.conf. This is ~200-300 lines of code — musl's approach is a good reference. Target: frankenlibc_core::resolv::dns module.", "acceptance_criteria": "DNS stub sends well-formed A/AAAA queries via UDP. Parses responses correctly. Handles CNAME (1 level). Retries on timeout. Unit tests with canned DNS response bytes. /etc/resolv.conf parsing tests.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-31T17:45:53.015187713Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-02T03:29:45.084643515Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-02T03:29:45.084346533Z", "close_reason": "Already implemented: native_dns_resolve() with UDP queries, ResolverConfig parsing /etc/resolv.conf, A/AAAA records, retries+timeouts, search domain logic. Zero host delegation.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["dns", "native", "resolv"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-zh1y.7.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-zh1y.7", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-31T17:45:53.015187713Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-zh1y.7.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-zh1y.7.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-31T17:45:53.427595176Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-zh1y.7.3", "title": "Wire full native getaddrinfo: numeric + hosts + DNS + AI_* flags", "description": "Combine numeric parsing + /etc/hosts + DNS stub into complete getaddrinfo. Apply AI_PASSIVE, AI_CANONNAME, AI_V4MAPPED, AI_ADDRCONFIG flags. Implement native freeaddrinfo to free our allocated addrinfo list. Remove all resolve_host_symbol_raw from resolv_abi.rs.", "acceptance_criteria": "Zero resolve_host_symbol_raw in resolv_abi.rs.
